title: How a Heat Wave Disturbs Generations of This Sex-Changing Spider
gist: A Molecular Ecology study shows a brief heat wave can disrupt Wolbachia bacteria that turn male dwarf spider offspring into females, upsetting sex ratios that the maternally inherited microbe manipulates for its own transmission.

insights.claim: Dwarf spiders host maternally inherited Wolbachia bacteria that ensure infected hosts produce mostly daughters by transforming potential male offspring into females.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Classic example of a microbe hijacking host sex determination.

insights.claim: A Molecular Ecology study finds that something as simple as a brief heat wave can disrupt the male/female balance of dwarf spiders by foiling the sex-changing bacteria.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Shows temperature as a lever on symbiont-driven sex ratios, with climate implications.
insights.locator: something as simple as a heat wave
